differential_diagnoses: Paraneoplastic erythroderma secondary to pulmonary malignancy
Severe erythrodermic atopic dermatitis with secondary superinfection
Cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (Mycosis fungoides, erythrodermic phase)
Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS syndrome)
next_steps: Flow cytometry and T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies on peripheral blood
Excisional lymph node biopsy for architectural analysis and immunophenotyping
Image-guided core needle or surgical biopsy of the pulmonary mass
Repeat skin biopsy with immunohistochemistry (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD30, CD25, FoxP3) to look for atypical lymphocytic infiltrates
Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate for staging
PET/CT scan to assess metabolic activity of nodal and pulmonary lesions
Close monitoring for secondary systemic infections while continuing immunosuppression-sparing therapies
